emergency_definition: And — this is new since the demo — we also want to treat any electrical issue at a medical facility as emergency, even if it's not life-threatening

emergency_routing_rules.fallback: Take message (name, number, address, issue description) and assure callback

non_emergency_routing_rules.after_hours: Collect caller information; Promise callback next business day; Promise callback by 9 AM; Do not transfer after hours

call_transfer_rules.failure_message: I apologize, I wasn't able to connect you. Let me take your information and someone will call you back shortly.

integration_constraints: For now, don't create any jobs in ServiceTrade automatically
special_rules: And — this is new since the demo — we also want to treat any electrical issue at a medical facility as emergency, even if it's not life-threatening
David Rodriguez: Yes — if a caller mentions a permit inspection or a city inspector, route that to Jenny directly, even after hours
greeting: Thank you for calling Apex Electrical Services, this is Clara. How can I help you?
after_hours_flow_summary:
1. Greet caller
2. Ask purpose of call
3. Determine if emergency or non-emergency
4. Emergency triggers: Complete power outage, Sparking, Exposed live wires...
5. Emergency: collect name, number, address -> transfer chain: on-call (480-555-0300) -> David Rodriguez (480-555-0155)
6. If transfer fails: take message, assure callback
7. Non-emergency: collect info, promise callback next business day
8. Ask 'Is there anything else I can help with?'
9. Close call
office_hours_flow_summary:
1. Greet caller
2. Ask purpose of call
3. Collect caller name and callback number
4. Transfer to: dispatch (480-555-0100) -> None (480-555-0101)
5. If transfer fails: take message, confirm follow-up
6. Ask 'Is there anything else I can help with?'
7. Close call
